message:
fcp-mesh: update callers in node unit tests and mesh_integration for the signed handle_decode_status / handle_symbol_ack signatures

Follow-up to the signature-verification commit. The existing test
call sites were still using the pre-signature signatures
(`handle_decode_status(&status, ts)` and
`handle_symbol_ack(&ack, ts)` with no peer and no signed input),
which fails the new trait bound that these handlers require a
signed message from a registered peer.

Changes:

* crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs (mod tests):
  - `handle_decode_status_with_missing_hint`: construct an
    `Ed25519SigningKey`, register its verifying key on the node
    under `NodeId("peer-1")` via `register_peer_signing_key`, make
    the `DecodeStatus` mutable, call `status.sign(&signing_key)` so
    the transcript produces a real signature, and call
    `node.handle_decode_status(&peer, &status, 2000)` with an
    `.expect("status should verify")`. The previous test only
    asserted "should not panic" — the updated test now proves the
    end-to-end verify path accepts a correctly-signed status with
    a missing_hint, rather than just smoke-testing that the fn
    returns at all.

* crates/fcp-mesh/tests/mesh_integration.rs:
  - `mod meshnode` decode-status scenario: register a peer signing
    key for `"peer-1"`, build the `DecodeStatus` with `mut`, call
    `status.sign(&signing_key)`, dispatch via
    `node.handle_decode_status(&peer, &status, 0).expect(...)`,
    then continue with the symbol-request-rejected assertions.
  - `mod meshnode` symbol-ack scenario: same pattern with
    `SymbolAck::new(...).sign(&signing_key)` followed by
    `node.handle_symbol_ack(&peer, &ack, 0).expect(...)`.
  - `mod real_component_integration` decode-status scenario (phase-2
    "follow-up request rejected" after a decode status): register
    the peer key, sign the status, dispatch through the new API,
    and keep the phase-3 assertions intact.

No production-code changes in this commit; it is strictly the test-
side migration to the now-mandatory signed-input API.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
